evaluations.title: Title 3
evaluations.comment: peiukene - peigmees, not little boy in any case, a groom! Poisikene - dear son, not little lad. Throws all of the song off.

evaluations.title: Title 4
evaluations.comment: This is a rare song that has very little dialectal words, although not purely in Estonian either.

evaluations.title: Title 6
evaluations.comment: The meaning is totally lost, but the words are not written in a grammatically correct way, so for instance kirst is written kerst, there are many letters missing here and there and the meaning is only understandable  when the text is read aloud. Heavily dialectal, and poetic additions to words.

evaluations.title: Title 9
evaluations.comment:
alvad -> halvad

In many songs the words are written as they were pronounced. The h is barely heard in this word so it's just omitted. May be actually the machine translation would cope better if it were listening to the song, but when the words are coded into tokens it just has no idea.

evaluations.title: Pulmalaul
evaluations.comment: The automatic translation again struggles with the words signifying relationships and omitted letters: mini - minia. arakas - harakas.

evaluations.title: Title 13
evaluations.comment: This one is surprisingly good! Again, ari -> hari is where the translation stumbles, but otherwise in some other places it did not stumble (valge'ed -> valget)

evaluations.title: Title 3
evaluations.comment: I already reviewed an almost identical one.
